name: Hong Kong
summary: First-mover Asian regime with full statutory licensing. HKD-referenced stablecoins prioritised; 1:1 high-quality liquid reserves; segregated trust accounts; T+1 redemption baseline.

name: Singapore
framework: Single-Currency Stablecoin (SCS) Framework under Payment Services Act
summary: Targets single-currency stablecoins pegged to SGD or G10 currencies. Reserve, capital, redemption (5 business days) and disclosure rules; MAS-regulated label restricted to compliant issuers.

name: European Union
framework: Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) — EMT & ART regimes
summary: EMT for e-money tokens pegged to single fiat; ART for asset-referenced tokens. Most comprehensive regime globally — passporting across 27 member states, strict reserve, redemption-at-par, and significant-token thresholds.

name: United Arab Emirates
framework: Payment Token Services Regulation + VARA Virtual Asset Rulebooks
summary: Fragmented across CBUAE (federal), VARA (Dubai), and FSRA (ADGM). Payment Token Services Regulation issued 2024; AED-referenced stablecoins prioritised. Multiple sandbox tracks active.

name: United States
regulator: OCC / NYDFS / state regulators / pending federal
framework: State trust charters + GENIUS Act / STABLE Act (pending federal)
summary: No unified federal regime. NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter remains primary path (Paxos, Gemini). GENIUS Act and STABLE Act under congressional consideration. Significant regulatory uncertainty.

name: Japan
summary: First major economy to regulate stablecoins under banking-equivalent rules. Issuance restricted to licensed banks, fund transfer service providers, and trust companies. JPY-referenced focus.
